title: waltheria acapulcensis
intro: Waltheria acapulcensis, also known as the Mexican Waltheria, is a plant species belonging to the family Malvaceae, commonly found in tropical regions of Central America and Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic compounds. The health benefits associated with Waltheria acapulcensis include its potential to treat various conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its ability to act as an antioxidant and antimicrobial agent. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which can be taken internally or applied topically to the affected area.
